receipt: launch-rehearsal ring — 6-box EU, real MiniMax-M2.5 on real 5090s via the stranger daemon path
goal: mimic real strangers joining at launch as faithfully as possible on VAST; find pain points before real people do; measure real performance. Test orchestrator only — never prod, never npm.
spend: ~$8 total (1 preflight box + 6 ring boxes, ~1.5h); balance $16.83 after
method: 3-agent design panel (realism/perf/provisioning) -> build fixes -> 1-box engine preflight -> 6-box full ring. Faithful stranger path: built worker shipped as a tarball (scp not npx, the one deviation), self-provisions engine+venv+sidecar+manifest+pull with zero env beyond the manifest pubkey pin + announce.

bugs_found_and_fixed.bug: daemon never passed sidecar -announce, so behind docker/NAT the sidecar advertised only its container-internal addr (172.17.x.x); ringmates dialed an unroutable private IP and the ring silently never formed
bugs_found_and_fixed.fix: c0mpute #51 — announce the detected public addr (C0MPUTE_SHARD_ANNOUNCE override for docker port-maps)
bugs_found_and_fixed.validated: the ring FORMED across 6 EU boxes; a box announced /ip4/<pub>/tcp/<mapped> and reached stage READY

bugs_found_and_fixed.bug: a stranger's --mode shard launched stage/coordinator WITHOUT graph-aux (CUDA-graph/static-KV/SDPA), so real rings would serve at the ~2x-slower no-graph floor
bugs_found_and_fixed.fix: c0mpute #51 — default the bit-exact graph trio on, operator env wins
bugs_found_and_fixed.validated: boxes launched with the defaults; tok/s effect UNMEASURED (see serve gap)

bugs_found_and_fixed.bug: the engine computes tok_s/mean_accept/g/transport-split then dropped them from SHARD_JOB_DONE — the daemon serve path was unmeasurable
the sim SimSeam.plan could not tile 62 layers across 6 stages (62=2x31: even split drops layers or overshoots to null)
manifest must be served BYTE-VERBATIM (the mf1 CID is over exact bytes); the sim re-serialized it (JSON.parse->stringify) -> CID mismatch -> pull fail-closed
bugs_found_and_fixed.validated: pull passed CID after the fix

bugs_found_and_fixed.bug: the daemon's OWN resolveManifest re-serialized the fetched doc (res.json()->JSON.stringify) before writing it, breaking the very --manifest-cid check it later runs. My CID pinning and my manifest resolver were mutually incompatible — EVERY launch pull would have failed closed
bugs_found_and_fixed.fix: c0mpute #51 — keep the raw bytes, parse only a copy for the pin/version guards, write verbatim
bugs_found_and_fixed.validated: manifest resolved verbatim -> 35GB verified pull -> stage loaded

bugs_found_and_fixed.bug: a cold cohort all joining at once triggers a re-form STORM: eager auto-form fires the moment minStages announce, but boxes take ~30min to pull+load; any disconnect DEGRADES the swarm and re-forms, preempting every in-progress pull so nothing stabilizes
bugs_found_and_fixed.fix: PARTIAL — c0mpute #51 --debounce lets nodes settle before forming; a real mitigation (don't re-form while stages are mid-pull, formation cooldown) is a follow-up
bugs_found_and_fixed.validated: reproduced; the storm is what starved the serve

proven_green_on_real_hardware: stranger self-provision: git-clone engine + pinned torch-2.11/vLLM/cutlass venv builds cleanly on a fresh 5090 box (zero env beyond the pubkey pin)
sidecar-v0.1.0 released binary downloads + sha-verifies from a real box
engine LOADS + RUNS on the 5090's sm_120: 'moe_backend auto -> cutlass (sm_120)', SHARD_STAGE_READY, 29.6GB/32.6GB VRAM (12 real layers)
network manifest resolution (verbatim) -> --manifest-cid + pubkey + expect-* all pass
verified pull of 35GB real weights (18 files, all hash-checked vs the signed manifest)
standby seeding + verified PEER pull: the seed box SERVED weight blocks over libp2p to the overlapping-range stage (torrent path, not HF)
the -announce fix: boxes advertise their real public addr; the ring FORMS across 6 EU boxes (SE/DK/CZ)

NOT_achieved.end_to_end_serve_tokens: The ring formed and the perf suite dispatched, but every job returned 0 TOKENS (warmup showed real 550ms/1350ms TTFT then empty; settlement rejected — no stage signed a receipt). Root cause: the ring never STABILIZED (the re-form storm, bug #7, meant stages/tunnels were still settling when the suite dispatched), and possibly a coordinator-drives-tokens-over-a-real-multi-hop-WAN-ring gap that needs isolating. So NO tok/s / TTFT / context numbers were captured.
NOT_achieved.why_stopped: the sim relaunch fought a shell-env bug repeatedly while 7 boxes burned; the disciplined call was to bank the findings and tear down rather than chase a stable serve at negative return.

fidelity.faithful: real self-provision, real measured admission (shard.probe on a real 5090), real signed-manifest verified pull, real libp2p transport + relay discovery + DHT/blockx seeding, real cross-EU ring formation, real sm_120 engine
fidelity.approximated: NAT ~ port-mapped VAST box (not residential CGNAT); strangers ~ boxes we rent (homogeneous 5090s); delivery ~ tarball not npx
fidelity.cannot_test_here: true residential/WSL2 (leyten's Ghent box); npx from the public registry (publish-gated); real human UX

next: A focused follow-up: stabilize the ring (the debounce + a 'no re-form while pulling' guard), get all 6 stages solidly READY, then isolate the coordinator serve over the real multi-hop ring to produce the first real tok/s numbers. All the hard prerequisites (engine on sm_120, verified pull, seeding, formation) are now proven; the remaining gap is the leg-8 serve stability over a real WAN ring + the churn-storm mitigation.
